message:
feat(runtime_compat): implement asupersync oneshot channel wrapper types

Adds a full compatibility layer for asupersync oneshot channels that
preserves the tokio-compatible Sender::send(value) API, so the ~30
respond.send(result) call sites in storage.rs and other modules need
no changes during the async runtime migration.

Key design decisions:
- Sender acquires Cx internally via Cx::current() (falling back to
  for_request()) for the asupersync two-phase reserve/commit send,
  keeping the fire-and-forget send(value) signature callers expect
- Receiver deliberately does NOT impl Future under asupersync —
  callers must go through oneshot_recv() which handles Cx acquisition
- cfg(feature = "asupersync-runtime") gates the new wrapper; the
  tokio path remains untouched as a fallback
- RecvError compatibility type matches tokio's error interface

Also updates the broadcast module docs to explicitly document the three
missing asupersync broadcast APIs (try_recv, receiver_count, len) that
block broadcast migration, replacing the vague "per-module migration
beads will update" language with concrete blockers.

Updates mux_client.rs tests to route oneshot receives through the
oneshot_recv() helper instead of bare .await, which is required because
the asupersync Receiver wrapper no longer impls Future. All 6 affected
test sites (shutdown/cancellation assertion patterns) now use
crate::runtime_compat::oneshot_recv(closed_rx) consistently.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
